data.title: ChargePoint CEO buys nearly $250K in company shares
data.description:
• ChargePoint (CHPT) president & chief executive officer Richard Wilmer bought 46,847 shares of common stock in a transaction worth approximately $250K, according to a regulatory filing.
  • The shares were purchased in multiple transactions at prices ranging from $5.02 to $5.45 on April 13, 2026.
  • ChargePoint (CHPT) shares were up 6% premarket at $5.76.

data.title: JPMorgan in charts: Commercial & investment bank up 19% Y/Y, AWM +11%, and CCB +7% in Q1
Wells Fargo trades lower after mixed Q1 earnings results, NII below estimates
data.description:
Wells Fargo (WFC) was trading lower after posting mixed earnings results for the first quarter of 2026 as net interest income fell short of expectations.

Shares were 2.12% lower at $84.80 before the market open on Tuesday.

The banking giant reported Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.60, which beats analyst estimates by $0.02, and revenue of $21.45B (+6.5% Y/Y), which misses consensus by $340M.

Revenue benefited from a 5% year-over-year increase in net interest income, which came to $12.10B for the quarter. However, the metric fell short of the average analyst estimate. The Cap IQ estimate for Q1 NII was $12.30B.

The consumer banking and lending segment saw a 7% year-over-year rise in quarterly revenue to $10.00B. Commercial banking saw its revenue increase to $3.12B from $3.08B.

Corporate and investment banking revenue was up 4% to $5.28B. Meanwhile, wealth and investment management advanced 14% to $3.88B.

Provision for credit losses jumped 21.78% from the prior year to $1.14B. The figure comes slightly below the $1.17B Cap IQ estimate for loan loss provision.

Common equity Tier 1 ratio stood at 10.3% at March 31, modestly below 10.6% at the end of the prior quarter. Supplementary leverage ratio fell to 5.9% from 6.2%.

"While markets have been volatile, we still see continued resiliency in the underlying economy and the financial health of the consumers and businesses we serve remains strong, though the impact of higher oil prices will likely take some time to materialize," said CEO Charlie Scharf.

"We will continue to monitor trends and respond accordingly, and we are well positioned to support our customers across a range of economic scenarios," added Scharf.

Earnings conference call is scheduled to take place today at 10:00 AM ET.

data.title: CarMax slides in early investor reaction to FQ4 earnings
data.description:
CarMax (KMX) fell in early trading on Tuesday despite topping consensus estimates with its fiscal fourth-quarter earnings report.

Retail used unit sales decreased 0.8%, and comparable store used unit sales declined 1.9% during the quarter. Total retail used vehicle revenues decreased 1.2% compared with the prior year's fourth quarter, driven by lower retail used units sold and a decrease in average retail selling price of approximately $110 per unit, or 0.4%.

Wholesale units increased 3.0% during the quarter. Total wholesale revenues declined 0.1% compared with the prior year’s fourth quarter due to a decrease in the average wholesale selling price of approximately $270 per unit, or 3.3%, mostly offset by the increase in wholesale units sold.

Total gross profit was down 9.4% to $605.3M. Retail used vehicle gross profit decreased 9.6%, and retail gross profit per used unit was $2,115, down $207 from last year’s record fourth quarter, reflecting pricing actions to drive an improved sales trend. Wholesale vehicle gross profit decreased 7.3% from a year ago, reflecting lower gross profit per unit, which declined $105 to $940 per unit, partially offset by higher wholesale unit volume.

CarMax (KNX) reported Q4 non-GAAP EPS of $0.34 vs. $0.64 a year ago and $0.21 consensus.

During Q4, CarMax (KMX) opened one new store location in Florence, Kentucky, and one stand-alone reconditioning/auction center located in Frederick, Maryland.

"We are moving with urgency to improve execution, drive efficiencies, and sharpen our customer offering," stated CarMax (KMX) CEO Keith Barr. "We will make CarMax the obvious choice for customers through competitive pricing, access to a large selection of high-quality vehicles, and an exceptional end-to-end customer experience. Together with CarMax’s brand and culture, our national scale positions us well to regain momentum and deliver the growth and returns this business is capable of generating," he added.

Looking ahead, CarMax (KMX) plans to open four new stores, two stand-alone reconditioning/auction centers, and two stand-alone auction facilities. The auto retailer expects capital expenditures of approximately $400M in FY27, largely reflecting spending to support its future long-term growth in offsite reconditioning and auction facilities, as well as new stores.

Shares of CarMax (KMX) fell 8.6% in premarket action.

data.title: Power stocks with AI tailwinds - Goldman
data.description:
Among secular growth themes, Goldman Sachs' equity team says it continues to think companies tied to investment in power infrastructure are a particularly attractive area.

The table below shows power stocks highlighted by Goldman equity analysts that should benefit from the secular tailwind of AI investment. 

"Future global investment in power infrastructure incentivized by the conflict in the Middle East could prove to be another tailwind for the theme," strategist David Kostin said. "In contrast with many other secular growth stocks, this group of stocks has outperformed sharply YTD."

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Johnson & Johnson tops Q1 expectations; boosts full-year outlook and increases quarterly dividend
data.description: *Other Operating Data Consensus Source: Bloomberg

data.title: CarMax Non-GAAP EPS of $0.34 beats by $0.13, revenue of $5.95B beats by $220M
data.description:
• CarMax press release (KMX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.34 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $5.95B (-0.8% Y/Y) beats by $220M.
  • Combined retail and wholesale unit sales of 303,969, an increase of 0.7%.
  • Bought 270,000 vehicles from consumers and dealers, an increase of 0.4%.
    • 229,000 vehicles were purchased from consumers, up 2.5%
    • 41,000 vehicles were purchased through dealers, down 9.5%
  •  
  • Shares -2% PM.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase GAAP EPS of $5.94 beats by $0.48, revenue of $50.5B beats by $1.59B
data.description:
• JPMorgan Chase press release (JPM): Q1 GAAP EPS of $5.94 beats by $0.48.
  • Revenue of $50.5B (+9.8% Y/Y) beats by $1.59B.
  • Net revenue was $50.5 billion, up 10%. Net interest income was $25.5 billion, up 9%. Noninterest revenue was $25.1 billion, up 11%.
  • The provision for credit losses was $2.5 billion. Net charge-offs were $2.3 billion, down $16 million. The net reserve build was $191 million, and included a $327 million net build in Wholesale and a $139 million net release in Consumer. In the prior year, the provision was $3.3 billion, net charge-offs were $2.3 billion and the net reserve build was $973 million.
  • Shares +1.4% PM.

data.title: BTIG favors Mondelez, Smucker, and Utz as it launches food sector coverage
data.description:
BTIG initiated coverage on the food sector with a heavy mix of Neutral ratings due to the challenging backdrop. 

Analyst Rob Dickerson highlighted that sector multiples are discounted on a historic basis for many companies in the sector because of sector demand, cost, and leverage concerns. Looking ahead, Dickerson and his team think valuations will stay depressed without improved core business demand, revenue growth, boosted margins, and capital structures.

Food stocks that BTIG started off coverage with a Neutral rating included BellRing Brands (BRBR), Campbell Soup (CPB), Conagra Brands (CAG), General Mills (GIS), Hershey (HSY), Kraft Heinz (KHC), McCormick (MKC), Post Holdings (POST), and Simply Good Foods (SMPL).

BTIG did find some three food stocks to recommend on what the firm views as safer, more defensive, cost-tailwind, cash step-up names amid an otherwise challenging operating backdrop.

Mondelez (MDLZ) is rated Buy because BTIG sees best-in-class global positioning and expects cocoa deflation from 2027 to drive significant margin expansion, above-algorithm EPS growth, and a strong low–double-digit free cash flow CAGR, leaving the current 17x forward P/E as overly discounted versus its growth and quality. J.M. Smucker (SJM) also landed a Buy rating as BTIG believes coffee profit expansion from lower arabica prices and tariff add-backs is underappreciated, supporting 20%+ segment EBIT growth next year and earnings above its high-single-digit algorithm and consensus at an attractive ~8x forward EBITDA. Meanwhile, Utz Brands (UTZ) is rated Buy because BTIG sees differentiated sales, profit, and free cash flow potential that is not fully reflected in its valuation, with around 32% implied upside to the $10 price target and robust projected total

data.description:
• Wells Fargo press release (WFC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.60 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $21.45B (+6.5% Y/Y) misses by $340M.
